mnemonics: K-NN: Keep Nearest Neighbors in mind. Think of it like asking your closest neighbors for advice - the majority vote decides the answer.
keyPoints: K-Nearest Neighbors (K-NN) is a simple, instance-based learning algorithm for classification and regression
The algorithm uses a distance metric (usually Euclidean) to find the 'k' most similar training examples to a new data point
For classification, the new point is assigned to the class most common among its k nearest neighbors
commonMistakes: Forgetting to normalize/scale features, which can cause features with larger ranges to dominate the distance calculation
Choosing an inappropriate value for k - too small leads to overfitting/noise sensitivity, too large leads to underfitting/oversmoothing
